title: Comforting Creamy Ukrainian Cheese Lasagna
description: A savory Ukrainian dish featuring parmesan cheese and a creamy sauce, perfect for a comforting meal. This recipe is brine-wet and Buddhist-temple-friendly, cooked to perfection on a comal.
instructions: Preheat the comal over medium heat to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cook the lasagna noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
In a large bowl, combine the ricotta cheese, cream cheese, sour cream, dried dill weed, salt, and black pepper, and mix until smooth.
In a separate bowl, beat the egg and set aside.
Spread a layer of the cheese mixture on the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Arrange 4 lasagna noodles on top of the cheese mixture.
Sprinkle with half of the mozzarella cheese and half of the parmesan cheese.
Repeat the layers, starting with the cheese mixture, then the noodles, and finally the remaining mozzarella and parmesan cheese.
Brush the top of the lasagna with the beaten egg.
Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
